executiveSummary.title: Executive Summary
executiveSummary.subtitle: ACME Corp Brand Measurement Intelligence · Q1 2026

smVolChart.title: Social Media Monthly Volume
smVolChart.subtitle: Posts by month
smVolChart.takeaway: February peak driven by product launch announcement. March returned to baseline as campaign settled.

tmVolChart.title: Traditional Media Monthly Volume
tmVolChart.subtitle: Articles by month
tmVolChart.takeaway: TM volume stable with modest February lift tied to launch coverage and analyst commentary.

signals.title: Product Launch
signals.desc: ACME X1 product launch in February drove a 46% spike in SM volume and significant earned media coverage.

signals.title: AI Integration
signals.desc: ACME's announced AI-powered platform upgrade elevated innovation perception among tech and enterprise media.

signals.title: Supply Chain Risk
signals.desc: Q1 supply disruption in APAC attracted negative coverage in trade media and social skepticism.

working: Product launch generated strong earned media with 41% SM positive rate
AI narrative resonating with enterprise and tech press audiences
LinkedIn engagement rate outperforming industry average at 8.2 interactions/post
notWorking: Supply chain narrative creating persistent negative tail in TM (3.2% negative)
Instagram and TikTok severely underutilized — combined 4% of posts, minimal engagement

risks.text: Supply chain disruption could escalate if APAC issues persist into Q2
Competitor X2 launch in April may absorb earned media share of voice

opportunities.text: AI platform story is uncrowded — window to own the narrative before competitors
LinkedIn professional audience is highly engaged and underserved with depth content

insights.label: Pattern
insights.text: February peaked at 510 posts (+46% vs January) driven by the ACME X1 product launch on Feb 8. Volume returned to 340 in March as launch coverage normalized.

insights.label: Platform
insights.text: LinkedIn accounts for 42% of total volume, followed by X/Twitter at 28%, Instagram at 18%, and YouTube at 12%. LinkedIn is the dominant professional engagement channel.

insights.label: Driver
insights.text: Launch announcement, partnership news, and analyst coverage drove the February spike. Organic community conversation sustained March volume above January baseline.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: Consistent content cadence between launch events could smooth volume curves. A monthly editorial calendar with 2-3 owned thought leadership pieces per week would maintain 400+ posts/month.

kpi-reach.title: SM Total Reach: 142M
kpi-reach.sub: Cumulative impression reach across all SM channels

insights.label: Composition
insights.text: LinkedIn contributes ~60M impressions; X/Twitter ~45M; Instagram and YouTube the remainder. LinkedIn reach correlates to professional/enterprise buyer audience.

insights.label: Context
insights.text: 142M impressions across 1,200 posts averages 118,333 impressions per post — well above industry median of 60,000 for mid-cap brands, indicating strong follower base quality.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: TikTok and YouTube are absent from top reach drivers despite high per-post engagement potential. Adding short-form video could add 20-30M incremental reach per quarter.

insights.label: Risk
insights.text: Reach concentration on LinkedIn creates dependency. If LinkedIn algorithm changes reduce organic reach (as has occurred in 2024-2025), total SM reach would drop materially.

insights.label: Volume Context
insights.text: 8,500 articles over 90 days is strong for a mid-cap company — approximately 94 articles per day. This reflects solid financial media footprint and active product news flow.

insights.label: Channel
insights.text: Online outlets represent 68% of volume, followed by wire services at 22%, and trade publications at 10%. Trade coverage — though smallest in volume — has highest audience quality.

insights.label: Earned vs Syndicated
insights.text: Estimated 65% syndicated wire content vs 35% earned editorial. Increasing the earned editorial share is the key narrative leverage opportunity.

insights.label: Pattern
insights.text: February spike (3,100 articles) closely tracks the product launch press cycle. January and March reflect structural/syndication-level baseline (~2,700 articles).

insights.label: Scale
insights.text: 210M TM reach exceeds SM reach (142M) by 48%, consistent with traditional media's broader distribution. High-reach outlets (MSN, Yahoo Finance) disproportionately drive this figure.

insights.label: Average Reach
insights.text: Average 24,700 reach per article reflects a mix of high-reach aggregators and low-reach local/trade outlets. Median per-article reach is likely closer to 8,000-12,000.

insights.label: Quality Note
insights.text: Trade media articles (850 articles) carry the highest credibility per impression despite lower total reach. Prioritizing trade relationships yields outsized brand equity per reader.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: Securing 5 placements per quarter in Tier 1 outlets (WSJ, Forbes, TechCrunch) each at 5M+ reach would add 25M+ high-quality impressions and meaningfully shift narrative perception.

insights.label: Rate Context
insights.text: 41% positive rate is above average for mid-cap brands in the enterprise software/technology sector (industry norm: 28-35%). Product launch enthusiasm and AI narrative are key drivers.

insights.label: Key Drivers
insights.text: Positive sentiment concentrated around: ACME X1 launch coverage, AI platform announcement, enterprise customer testimonials shared on LinkedIn, and analyst upgrade on Feb 12.

insights.label: Negative Monitoring
insights.text: 4% negative (48 posts) is low overall but supply chain skepticism creates a concentrated negative thread. Proactive counter-messaging is recommended before Q2.

insights.label: Platform Differential
insights.text: LinkedIn likely 50%+ positive (professional community, brand advocates); X/Twitter likely more mixed given broader audience including skeptics and competitors.

insights.label: Pattern
insights.text: January: 350 posts (baseline). February: 510 posts (+46%). March: 340 posts (-33% from peak, essentially back to baseline). Classic launch spike with normal decay.

insights.label: Driver
insights.text: February spike driven by ACME X1 product launch (Feb 8), analyst upgrade (Feb 12), and enterprise customer announcement (Feb 19). Three compounding events in 12 days.

insights.label: Sustainability
insights.text: March at 340 posts (within 3% of January baseline) suggests launch enthusiasm was event-driven, not organic community growth. Sustained content program needed to prevent Q2 trough.

insights.label: Forecast
insights.text: April-May volume depends on whether follow-on milestones (customer wins, feature releases, industry conference presentations) are planned. Without new catalysts, volume may dip to 280-300 posts.

insights.label: Pattern
insights.text: Jan: 2,800 · Feb: 3,100 (+11%) · Mar: 2,600 (-16%). TM volume is far more stable than SM, reflecting structural syndication coverage baseline.

insights.label: Driver
insights.text: February uptick tied to launch press release syndication and analyst note distribution. Less volatile than SM because wire services distribute stories regardless of audience interest signals.

insights.label: Structural vs Earned
insights.text: Month-to-month stability (±15%) suggests the majority of TM coverage is structural syndication. Earned editorial coverage would show sharper peaks and troughs aligned to news events.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: Build a proactive media relations program targeting 10 Tier 1 journalists. Converting 5% of monthly volume from syndicated to earned editorial would significantly improve brand narrative quality.

insights.label: Pattern
insights.text: The Feb 8 launch generated the single-largest SM and TM volume spike of Q1. Social volume was 510 posts in February vs. 350 in January — a 46% increase.

insights.label: Coverage Quality
insights.text: Launch coverage spanned enterprise tech media (TechCrunch, ZDNet, CRN), financial media (Yahoo Finance, MarketWatch), and LinkedIn thought leadership — a strong multi-channel footprint.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: The launch narrative is still fresh. Q2 follow-on content (customer case studies, performance benchmarks, analyst deep dives) can extend the earned media lifecycle by 60-90 days.

insights.label: Risk
insights.text: Supply chain concerns surfaced in the same month as launch, creating a mixed narrative moment. Ensuring supply issues are visibly resolved before Q2 reviews is critical to protecting launch momentum.

insights.label: Signal
insights.text: AI platform announcement in mid-February generated coverage in both enterprise tech media and general business press — a broader audience than typical product upgrade coverage.

insights.label: Narrative Value
insights.text: AI integration positions ACME alongside technology leaders in enterprise software. Competitor brands (CompetitorA, CompetitorB) have not yet made comparable AI announcements.

insights.label: Audience Resonance
insights.text: CTO and enterprise decision-maker audiences respond strongly to AI capabilities messaging. LinkedIn is the optimal channel — verified use-case posts receive 3-5× engagement vs generic product posts.

insights.label: Window
insights.text: ACME has an estimated 2-3 quarter window before competitors announce comparable AI roadmaps. Use this period to define the category terms and secure thought leadership positioning.

insights.label: Pattern
insights.text: Supply chain disruption mentioned in 8-12% of negative TM articles in Q1. Coverage concentrated in trade press (Supply Chain Dive, Logistics Management) and financial analyst notes.

insights.label: Risk Level
insights.text: Current risk level is moderate. Supply chain narratives are standard business reporting, not crisis-level. However, if Q2 earnings show revenue impact, TM negative rate could spike from 3.2% to 6-8%.

insights.label: Counter Strategy
insights.text: Proactive mitigation: issue a supplier diversification update in April, highlighting APAC recovery timeline and contingency measures. Show investors and press the problem is being managed.

insights.label: Monitoring
insights.text: Set real-time alerts for 'ACME supply chain,' 'ACME shortage,' 'ACME APAC.' If coverage reaches 15+ articles in a single week, activate rapid-response communications.

exec-working.title: What's Working: Key Strengths
exec-working.sub: Momentum drivers across SM and TM

insights.label: Launch Momentum
insights.text: Product launch generated strong earned media across enterprise tech, financial, and trade press — a multi-audience footprint rare for a single announcement.

insights.label: AI Narrative
insights.text: AI platform positioning is generating positive coverage in non-traditional channels (tech press, innovation media) that expand ACME's audience beyond core enterprise buyers.

insights.label: LinkedIn Efficiency
insights.text: LinkedIn is generating 8.2 interactions per post — above the enterprise software benchmark of 6.0. Professional audience is engaged and advocates for ACME content.

insights.label: Supply Chain Narrative
insights.text: APAC disruption is creating a persistent negative thread in TM that is undermining the positive launch and AI stories. Two competing narratives in the same quarter dilutes brand momentum.

insights.label: Instagram and TikTok Gap
insights.text: Both platforms are severely underutilized. Combined, they represent 4% of SM posts. Younger enterprise decision-makers and technical talent increasingly discover brands on these platforms.

insights.label: Neutral TM Dominance
insights.text: 76% of TM coverage is neutral/informational. ACME is being reported on factually but not celebrated. Converting even 5% of neutral coverage to positive requires proactive editorial engagement.

insights.label: Supply Chain Escalation
insights.text: If APAC supply issues persist into Q2 and affect revenue guidance, TM negative coverage could spike to 7-10%, materially shifting brand perception among investors and enterprise buyers.

insights.label: Competitor Launch
insights.text: CompetitorX is rumored to launch a competing product in April 2026. If their launch generates stronger SM volume and TM coverage, ACME's share of voice could erode by 20-30% by end of Q2.

insights.label: Narrative Dilution
insights.text: Running three simultaneous narratives (product launch, AI platform, supply chain) risks audience attention fragmentation. A focused communications hierarchy is needed for Q2.

insights.label: AI Category Ownership
insights.text: No direct competitor has established an 'AI-first enterprise platform' narrative. ACME has a 2-3 quarter window to own this positioning before the market becomes crowded.

insights.label: LinkedIn Depth Content
insights.text: LinkedIn engagement rate (8.2 int/post) signals an active, interested professional community. Deploying richer content — customer case studies, CTO perspective series, data-driven thought leadership — can compound engagement.

insights.label: Trade Media Investment
insights.text: Trade press coverage (enterprise tech, industry-specific publications) remains underdeveloped at 10% of TM volume. Targeted pitch programs for Tier 1 trade outlets would increase credibility per impression significantly.

volTrendChart.title: Monthly Post Volume Trend
volTrendChart.subtitle: Jan–Mar 2026 · Peak: February
volTrendChart.takeaway: February peak driven by ACME X1 launch. March decay indicates event-driven spike; sustained content strategy needed to maintain 400+ posts/month baseline.

sentimentChart.title: Sentiment Distribution
sentimentChart.subtitle: Overall sentiment split across all SM channels
sentimentChart.takeaway: 41% positive rate is above industry average. 54% neutral reflects informational content cadence. 4% negative tied primarily to supply chain coverage.

sentimentDrivers.title: Sentiment Drivers
sentimentDrivers.subtitle: Key themes behind positive and negative content
positive: ACME X1 product launch enthusiasm from enterprise customers
AI platform integration story resonating with tech-forward audience
Analyst upgrade Feb 12 generating investor community enthusiasm
negative: APAC supply chain disruption flagged by logistics and financial press
Competitive pricing pressure mentioned in enterprise buyer forums

platformBreakdown.title: Platform Breakdown
platformBreakdown.subtitle: Posts and interactions per platform
platformBreakdown.takeaway: LinkedIn drives highest absolute interactions. YouTube punches above its post volume (8.96 int/post vs LinkedIn's 8.2). Instagram at 8.0 int/post is competitive — increase posting frequency.

insights.text: Rank 2 sources (360 posts) are the largest contributor — primarily wire distribution and mid-tier tech press.
Only 240 posts from Rank 1 top-tier sources. Targeted Tier 1 outreach could double this in Q2.

insights.label: Volume Context
insights.text: 1,200 SM posts over 90 days averages 13.3 posts/day across all platforms. For an enterprise technology company of ACME's scale, this is competitive but has room to grow.

insights.label: Platform Mix
insights.text: LinkedIn 42% + X/Twitter 28% = 70% of volume. Instagram (18%) and YouTube (12%) represent genuine social engagement channels that are underutilized relative to their engagement rate performance.

insights.label: Benchmark
insights.text: Peer enterprise software companies at comparable revenue scale typically generate 1,800-2,500 social posts/quarter. ACME has room to increase owned content frequency by 50% without quality tradeoff.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: Establishing a consistent content calendar with 3-4 owned posts per day (LinkedIn, X, Instagram) would increase quarterly volume to ~1,800 posts while improving audience familiarity and algorithm reach.

insights.label: Composition
insights.text: LinkedIn contributes ~60M impressions on the strength of a large professional follower base. X/Twitter adds ~45M; Instagram ~25M; YouTube ~12M.

insights.label: Engagement Quality
insights.text: LinkedIn and YouTube audiences are high-intent — they follow ACME specifically. X/Twitter reach includes more passive/incidental exposure from hashtags and trends.

insights.label: Optimization
insights.text: Adding consistent YouTube content (3-4 videos/month) at current engagement velocity would add 15-20M reach per quarter with above-average interaction rates (8.96 int/post).

insights.label: Risk
insights.text: Heavy reliance on LinkedIn algorithm for organic reach is a platform dependency risk. Building email/newsletter audience as an owned channel provides algorithm-resilient reach.

insights.label: Jan Baseline
insights.text: 350 posts — normal operating cadence without major news catalysts. Consistent with Q4 2025 trailing average, suggesting healthy organic community activity.

insights.label: Feb Spike
insights.text: 510 posts — driven by three compounding events in 12 days: ACME X1 launch (Feb 8), analyst upgrade (Feb 12), enterprise customer partnership announcement (Feb 19).

insights.label: Mar Decay
insights.text: 340 posts — 33% decline from February peak, returning to near-January baseline. Normal post-launch decay; indicates conversation was primarily event-driven, not self-sustaining.

insights.label: Content Strategy
insights.text: Create a 'bridge content' program between event peaks: monthly product deep-dives, weekly feature spotlights, biweekly customer stories. Goal: maintain 400+ posts/month floor without relying on launch events.

insights.label: LinkedIn Dominance
insights.text: 504 posts (42%) — professional network delivering highest interaction count. Core channel for enterprise B2B storytelling, executive thought leadership, and analyst engagement.

insights.label: X/Twitter
insights.text: 336 posts (28%) — real-time news distribution and industry commentary. Key for breaking news amplification, conference live coverage, and engaging technology journalists.

insights.label: Instagram Gap
insights.text: 216 posts (18%) — present but underperforming relative to engagement rate (8.0 int/post). Increasing frequency to 5 posts/day would dramatically improve both reach and interactions from this channel.

insights.label: YouTube Opportunity
insights.text: 144 posts (12%) — highest per-post engagement rate at 8.96 int/post. Severely underutilized. 3-4 video posts/week would generate disproportionate engagement growth relative to effort.

insights.label: Positive (41%)
insights.text: 492 posts. Driven primarily by launch excitement, AI innovation story, and analyst upgrade. Enterprise customer testimonials on LinkedIn are the highest-quality positive content.

insights.label: Neutral (54%)
insights.text: 648 posts. Majority is informational — product feature announcements, event recaps, industry statistics shared without strong editorial voice. Converting 10% of neutrals to positive is the highest-leverage sentiment intervention.

insights.label: Negative (4%)
insights.text: 48 posts. Concentrated around supply chain narrative and competitive pricing concerns. Not a crisis, but requires proactive counter-messaging before the narrative consolidates.

insights.label: Platform Differential
insights.text: LinkedIn positive rate likely 48-52% (engaged professional advocates). X/Twitter likely 30-35% positive given broader, more critical audience. Platform-split sentiment analysis would sharpen targeting.

sm-pos-drivers.title: SM Sentiment Drivers
sm-pos-drivers.sub: What creates positive and negative content

insights.label: Top Positive
insights.text: ACME X1 launch enthusiasm, AI platform announcement, analyst upgrade (Feb 12), enterprise customer testimonials, industry award recognition in March.

insights.label: Top Negative
insights.text: APAC supply chain disruption (primary), competitive pricing pressure from emerging rivals, delayed shipping timelines mentioned in customer-facing posts.

insights.label: Neutral Composition
insights.text: Product feature announcements, industry event coverage, and company news that lacks a persuasive editorial angle. Adding a customer impact statement to each announcement shifts neutral to positive.

insights.label: Conversion Opportunity
insights.text: LinkedIn's 648 neutral posts contain significant conversion potential. Adding a customer outcome statistic or exec perspective sentence to each post could shift 15-20% from neutral to positive in 90 days.

insights.label: LinkedIn Efficiency
insights.text: LinkedIn = 42% of posts, 42% of interactions (8.2 int/post). Consistent performer — the platform delivers in proportion to its investment.

insights.label: YouTube Hidden Strength
insights.text: YouTube = 12% of posts but delivers 8.96 int/post — the highest rate across all platforms. Dramatically underutilized. 2× increase in YouTube frequency would likely drive 2× total interaction growth.

insights.label: Instagram Proportionate
insights.text: Instagram at 8.0 int/post is competitive. 18% of posts generating 17.6% of interactions is efficient. Increasing post frequency here has clear positive expected value.

insights.label: X/Twitter Scale
insights.text: X/Twitter at 8.0 int/post matches Instagram. As the highest-volume secondary platform, even small per-post improvements here would materially move total interaction count.

insights.label: LinkedIn (504 posts)
insights.text: 4,133 interactions = 8.2 int/post. Primary professional channel. Strong for executive content, technical deep-dives, and customer case studies targeting enterprise buyers and analysts.

insights.label: X/Twitter (336 posts)
insights.text: 2,688 interactions = 8.0 int/post. Real-time and news-reactive. Best for breaking news amplification, conference coverage, and engaging technology journalists and industry influencers.

insights.label: Instagram (216 posts)
insights.text: 1,728 interactions = 8.0 int/post. Healthy engagement rate but underpowered by post frequency. Visual product demos, team culture, and customer success imagery perform best here.

insights.label: YouTube (144 posts)
insights.text: 1,291 interactions = 8.96 int/post. Highest engagement rate — strongest ROI per post of any channel. Product walkthroughs, customer testimonials, and exec interviews drive exceptional engagement.

insights.label: Total Interactions
insights.text: 9,840 total interactions across 1,200 posts = 8.2 interactions per post average. This is above the enterprise software benchmark of 6.0-6.5 int/post, indicating a quality-engaged follower base.

insights.label: Channel Consistency
insights.text: All four platforms perform within a tight range (8.0-8.96 int/post). This consistency signals that content quality is uniformly strong, rather than one breakout channel masking underperformers.

insights.label: Growth Opportunity
insights.text: 9,840 interactions across 1,200 posts. Increasing post volume to 1,800/quarter while maintaining engagement rate would yield ~14,800 interactions — a 50% increase from volume alone.

insights.label: Action
insights.text: Test content formats systematically: A/B test carousel vs. single-image posts on LinkedIn, long vs. short video on YouTube, question-format vs. statement-format X/Twitter posts. Optimize toward highest-performing formats.

insights.label: Rank 1 (240 posts)
insights.text: Top-tier influencers, major publications, and verified enterprise accounts. These posts generate the highest downstream amplification and brand credibility signal.

insights.label: Rank 2 (360 posts)
insights.text: Wire services and mid-tier tech press — the largest category. Consistent distribution but moderate individual brand impact. Syndication layer for launch news.

insights.label: Ranks 3-4 (600 posts)
insights.text: Niche publications, regional outlets, individual professional voices. Important for long-tail topic coverage and reaching specific industry micro-communities.

insights.label: Strategy
insights.text: Prioritize outreach to 10-15 Rank 1 accounts: enterprise tech analysts, CTO/CISO-level influencers on LinkedIn, top-tier trade journalists. 10 Rank 1 advocates outperform 100 Rank 3 mentions in brand authority terms.

volTrendChart.title: Monthly Article Volume
volTrendChart.subtitle: Jan: 2,800 · Feb: 3,100 · Mar: 2,600
volTrendChart.takeaway: February peak aligns with product launch press cycle. Volume is structurally stable — wire syndication creates consistent coverage floor independent of news events.

insights.text: Online dominates (68%) — digital-first enterprise tech coverage landscape
Trade media (850 articles, 10%) carries highest audience quality for enterprise buyers

sentimentChart.title: Traditional Media Sentiment Split
sentimentChart.subtitle: 8,500 articles analyzed
sentimentChart.takeaway: 71% neutral reflects structural wire coverage. 5.8% positive is above industry average for enterprise tech. 3.2% negative tied primarily to supply chain coverage — actionable with proactive messaging.

narratives.title: Dominant TM Narratives
narratives.subtitle: Theme analysis from top publications

items.text: Product launch and innovation story dominating enterprise tech coverage (primary)
Analyst upgrades and revenue growth narrative building investor confidence
Supply chain disruption creating a persistent but manageable negative thread

narratives.takeaway: Innovation and product narrative leading TM coverage is a strong brand signal. Supply chain counter-messaging needed to prevent narrative contamination in Q2.

insights.text: Online/Web (5,780) = syndicated financial and tech wire content dominating volume
Trade (850) = high-value enterprise and industry-specific coverage

items.text: ACME X1 generating cross-sector enterprise tech coverage
Analyst responses predominantly positive
Feature coverage in TechCrunch and ZDNet anchoring narrative
Analyst upgrade Feb 12 generating downstream financial media pickup
Revenue growth guidance well-received in earnings commentary
Stock coverage broadly favorable with positive-to-neutral balance
APAC supply chain disruption covered in trade and logistics press
Competitive pricing pressure referenced in analyst commentary
Risk coverage concentrated in specialist outlets, not mainstream press

insights.label: Scale Context
insights.text: 8,500 articles over 90 days = 94 articles/day. For an enterprise technology company at ACME's scale, this reflects a solid media footprint with active product and financial news flow.

insights.label: Structural vs Earned
insights.text: Estimated 65% syndicated wire content vs 35% earned editorial. Improving the earned editorial ratio is the primary narrative leverage opportunity for Q2.

insights.label: Geographic Focus
insights.text: Coverage is predominantly US-centric with some UK and APAC pickup from international wire distribution. International earned media is a development opportunity.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: Target increasing earned editorial from 35% to 50% of total coverage by Q4 2026. This requires proactive journalist relationships with 15-20 Tier 1 enterprise tech reporters.

insights.label: Volume Driver
insights.text: High-reach digital outlets (Yahoo Finance: 30M+ UMV, MSN: 65M+ UMV) drive the reach concentration. A single major outlet placement can deliver more reach than 500 local articles.

insights.label: Average Reach
insights.text: Average 24,700 per article reflects a mix of high-reach aggregators and specialist trade outlets. Median per-article reach is likely 8,000-12,000.

insights.label: Quality Weighting
insights.text: TechCrunch (185 articles, ~8M UMV) and Yahoo Finance (310 articles, ~30M UMV) are the quality/reach sweet spot. These outlets deliver both credibility and audience scale.

insights.label: Action
insights.text: Build a monthly 'reach quality index': segment total reach by Tier 1 (top 20 publications), Tier 2 (trade media), Tier 3 (wire/local). Track and improve the Tier 1 share over time.

insights.label: Context
insights.text: 5.8% positive is above average for enterprise technology companies (industry norm: 3-5%). Product launch momentum and analyst upgrade are the primary drivers of above-baseline positive coverage.

insights.label: Positive Drivers
insights.text: Investment analyst upgrade (Feb 12), TechCrunch feature on ACME X1 innovation, enterprise customer case study coverage, and AI platform announcement in trade press.

insights.label: Benchmark Target
insights.text: With continued launch momentum and proactive trade media engagement, a 7-8% TM positive rate is achievable by Q3 2026. Target: 5 proactive earned editorial placements per month.

insights.label: Gap Opportunity
insights.text: SM generates 41% positive vs TM's 5.8% — a 7× gap that is structurally normal but indicates TM narrative management has significant headroom for improvement.

insights.label: Sources
insights.text: Supply chain disruption coverage (primary, ~60% of negative articles) concentrated in logistics and operations trade media. Competitive pricing pressure commentary accounts for ~30%.

insights.label: Risk Level
insights.text: 3.2% negative is at the upper threshold of normal for this industry. If supply chain issues persist and revenue impact is reported in Q2, negative rate could spike to 6-8%.

insights.label: Mitigation
insights.text: Issue a proactive supply chain update in April: announce mitigation measures, APAC recovery timeline, and diversification strategy. Proactive communication reduces negative article potential by 40-60%.

insights.label: Monitoring
insights.text: Set real-time alerts for 'ACME supply chain,' 'ACME shortage,' 'ACME delivery.' Threshold: if negative rate exceeds 5% in any single week, activate rapid-response communications within 48 hours.

insights.label: Jan Baseline
insights.text: 2,800 articles — structural coverage baseline. Wire syndication creates consistent article volume independent of specific news events. Q4 earnings follow-on coverage contributes.

insights.label: Feb Peak
insights.text: 3,100 articles (+11%) — product launch press release distribution drives the spike. Unlike SM which showed 46% increase, TM shows only 11% — reflecting the syndication machine's stability.

insights.label: Mar Decay
insights.text: 2,600 articles (-16% from February) — slightly below baseline, possibly reflecting a news-light post-launch period. Proactive media pitching in March could have maintained 2,800+ articles.

insights.label: Structural Insight
insights.text: TM volume stability (±15%) confirms that most coverage is wire-driven. Building proactive earned editorial relationships would create sharper peaks aligned to strategic news moments without losing the structural floor.

insights.label: Online Dominance
insights.text: 5,780 articles (68%) from online outlets. This reflects the digital-first media landscape for enterprise tech. Online is high-volume but moderate credibility per article.

insights.label: Trade Value
insights.text: 850 trade articles (10%) reach the highest-value audience for ACME: enterprise buyers, IT decision-makers, procurement professionals. Quality over quantity is the trade media thesis.

insights.label: Wire Architecture
insights.text: BusinessWire and PR Newswire distribution underpins the online volume. Each press release spawns 50-150 syndicated articles across aggregator sites.

insights.label: Optimization
insights.text: Increasing trade media coverage from 10% to 15% of total volume (an additional 425 articles) would require direct journalist pitching and potentially 1-2 dedicated enterprise tech PR resources.

insights.label: Neutral (71%)
insights.text: 6,035 articles. Structural wire and financial journalism defaults to factual, balanced reporting. This is industry-standard, not a brand failure. However, proactive story pitching can shift portions from neutral to positive.

insights.label: Positive (5.8%)
insights.text: 493 articles. Primary drivers: analyst upgrade coverage, product launch feature coverage, AI innovation stories, enterprise customer case studies. Above-average for the sector.

insights.label: Negative (3.2%)
insights.text: 272 articles. Supply chain narrative is the dominant driver. Concentrated in trade and logistics media rather than mainstream press — manageable with proactive counter-messaging.

insights.label: Strategic Target
insights.text: Q2 goal: positive rate to 7%, negative rate to 2%. Achievable through (1) supply chain proactive update, (2) 5 earned editorial pitches/month, (3) analyst briefing before Q2 earnings.

insights.label: Product Leadership
insights.text: ACME X1 launch coverage establishing 'next-generation enterprise platform' narrative. This is the most valuable narrative to sustain — each customer win story can extend it organically.

insights.label: Investment Story
insights.text: Analyst upgrade and revenue guidance coverage anchors positive investor narrative. Consistent investor communications cadence will maintain this framing through Q2.

insights.label: Supply Chain Risk
insights.text: The supply chain thread is negative but isolated to specialist outlets. Mainstream financial and tech press are not amplifying it — yet. Proactive resolution messaging keeps it contained.

insights.label: AI Opportunity
insights.text: AI integration story is underrepresented in TM relative to its SM resonance. Proactive pitching to AI-focused tech publications (The Verge, MIT Technology Review, Wired) would build TM AI narrative to match SM.

tm-pubs-detail.title: Top Publication Sources
tm-pubs-detail.sub: By article volume and strategic value

insights.label: BusinessWire (420)
insights.text: Primary wire distribution vehicle — high volume, moderate individual credibility. Ensures broad aggregator pickup of ACME press releases across 4,000+ outlets.

insights.label: Yahoo Finance (310)
insights.text: High-reach financial aggregator (30M+ UMV). Positive presence here builds investor community awareness. Analyst upgrade coverage here delivers maximum financial audience reach.

insights.label: TechCrunch (185)
insights.text: Highest editorial credibility for enterprise tech. 185 articles in Q1 is strong — indicates genuine editorial interest in ACME. Nurture this relationship with exclusive briefings.

insights.label: Supply Chain Dive (140)
insights.text: Trade outlet concentrated in supply chain coverage — ACME's negative narrative hub in Q1. A proactive resolution story pitched here would directly address the negative thread at its source.

insights.label: Online/Web (5,780)
insights.text: Digital-first coverage combining aggregator syndication and direct online editorial. High volume supports baseline brand visibility but limited editorial depth or narrative control.

insights.label: Trade (850)
insights.text: Enterprise tech, supply chain, and industry-specific trade publications. Despite lowest volume, delivers the highest-value audience. Priority for proactive pitching investment.

insights.label: News (1,870)
insights.text: Wire-distributed news content reaching regional and national outlets. Strong for geographic reach breadth; lower per-article brand narrative impact than earned editorial.

insights.label: Coverage Type
insights.text: Product reviews, launch announcements, feature analyses, and competitive comparisons. TechCrunch and ZDNet providing the editorial anchor pieces that drive credibility.

insights.label: Key Stories Q1
insights.text: ACME X1 launch announcement (Feb 8), TechCrunch editorial feature (Feb 11), enterprise customer partnership announcement (Feb 19), industry analyst validation commentary.

insights.label: Quality Premium
insights.text: Product launch coverage tends to appear in higher-credibility tech outlets. These articles have disproportionate influence on enterprise buyer consideration and analyst recommendations.

insights.label: Extension Strategy
insights.text: Customer case study pitching can extend the launch narrative through Q2-Q3. Aim for 2-3 new customer feature stories per month, each pitched exclusively to a Tier 1 tech publication.

insights.label: Coverage Drivers
insights.text: Analyst upgrade (Feb 12), quarterly earnings preview coverage, revenue guidance commentary, and institutional investor note syndication across financial media.

insights.label: Cascade Effect
insights.text: A single analyst upgrade generates 80-150 downstream articles across financial aggregators (Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat, Seeking Alpha). This is the TM syndication machine working positively for ACME.

insights.label: Risk
insights.text: Financial narrative is closely tied to stock performance and earnings results. If Q2 results show supply chain revenue impact, the same syndication machine would cascade negative financial coverage.

insights.label: Analyst Relations Priority
insights.text: Identify 3-5 'swing' analysts at mid-tier firms currently at Neutral. Targeted Q2 investor briefing could move 2-3 to Buy, generating another positive coverage wave with compounding effect.

insights.label: Concentration
insights.text: Supply chain risk coverage is concentrated in specialist trade outlets (Supply Chain Dive, Logistics Management, FreightWaves). Mainstream press has not amplified this narrative — a positive containment signal.

insights.label: Severity
insights.text: 272 negative articles over 90 days (3 articles/day) is elevated but not crisis-level. The ratio to positive articles (493) remains at 1.8:1 positive-to-negative — a healthy baseline.

insights.label: Response Strategy
insights.text: Pitch a proactive 'supply chain resilience' story to Supply Chain Dive and Logistics Management directly. Converting the negative hub outlets to neutral-to-positive immediately reduces the negative rate.

insights.label: Monitoring
insights.text: Track weekly: total negative article count vs 3-week rolling average. If volume increases >50% week-over-week, activate communications rapid-response protocol within 48 hours.

sentimentCompare.title: Sentiment Comparison: SM vs. TM
sentimentCompare.subtitle: Positive · Neutral · Negative rates
sentimentCompare.takeaway: SM generates 7× more positive sentiment than TM. Social channels are the brand's emotional amplifier — traditional media remains predominantly informational. Positive-to-negative ratio is healthy on both channels.

insights.text: Both channels peak in February — aligned to product launch announcement cycle
SM declines faster post-launch than TM (TM sustained via syndication machine)

reputation.title: Multi-Dimensional Reputation Scorecard
reputation.subtitle: Based on SM + TM signal analysis
reputation.scaleNote: Scored 1–10 based on SM and TM signal strength

scores.label: Innovation
scores.note: AI platform story generating cross-channel innovation narrative

scores.label: Financial Strength
scores.note: Analyst upgrade and revenue guidance driving positive investor narrative

scores.label: Product Quality
scores.note: X1 launch well-received; supply chain concerns create quality perception risk

scores.label: Customer Trust
scores.note: Customer testimonials present but supply chain narrative creates concern

scores.label: Brand Advocacy
scores.note: 41% SM positive signals engaged brand advocates; TM advocacy is developing

items.text: TM 7× more volume than SM (8,500 vs 1,200) — normal for enterprise technology companies
SM generates 9,840 interactions vs near-zero TM interactions — quality differential is wide
Both channels synchronized on February launch peak — strong narrative alignment signal
Customer voice present but not amplified — testimonials exist but aren't driving TM coverage
Supply chain narrative creating dissonance between positive launch story and operational concerns
AI platform story resonates strongly in SM but remains underdeveloped in TM
LinkedIn is primary owned channel for brand building — scale content investment immediately
Use TM-confirmed launch moments to amplify on SM within 6-12 hours for maximum impact
Trade media engagement converts directly to enterprise buyer consideration — prioritize TechCrunch and ZDNet relationships

insights.label: Aligned Themes
insights.text: Both SM and TM are consistently covering ACME X1 launch, analyst upgrade, and AI platform story. The core Q1 narrative is aligned across channels — a strong communications signal.

insights.label: Divergence
insights.text: SM has stronger positive sentiment (41% vs 5.8% TM) which is structural, not a gap. The AI narrative is more developed in SM than TM — a proactive TM pitch opportunity.

insights.label: Supply Chain Tension
insights.text: Supply chain coverage is more concentrated in TM (specialist trade outlets) than SM. This creates channel divergence on risk perception — enterprise buyers reading TM may have higher risk concern than SM audience.

insights.label: Integration Opportunity
insights.text: When TM breaks a product or customer story, use SM within 6-12 hours to amplify with owned content — executive LinkedIn post, customer quote, data visualization. Speed is the execution gap.

insights.label: The Gap
insights.text: SM generates 7× more positive sentiment than TM. This is a structural feature of media types — self-selected SM followers skew positive; TM audiences default to neutral.

insights.label: Brand Equity Location
insights.text: Brand emotional equity lives in SM (41% positive, engaged advocates). Brand factual credibility lives in TM (analyst coverage, product reviews). Both are necessary for comprehensive brand health.

insights.label: Influencing TM Sentiment
insights.text: To lift TM sentiment: (1) secure feature/profile editorial coverage, (2) provide compelling on-record customer success quotes, (3) generate industry award wins and recognition that journalists can report positively.

insights.label: Target
insights.text: A TM positive rate of 7-8% with 2:1 positive-to-negative ratio maintained is a realistic 12-month target that provides strong enterprise-grade brand credibility.

insights.label: Total Scale
insights.text: 352M combined impressions in Q1 2026. For a mid-cap enterprise technology company, this is a strong media presence — competitive with category leaders at similar revenue scale.

insights.label: Channel Quality
insights.text: SM reach quality is higher per impression (active professional audience, high engagement rate). TM reach is broader but includes aggregator audience with lower per-impression brand impact.

insights.label: Unique Audience
insights.text: Meaningful overlap between SM and TM audiences (analysts, investors, enterprise buyers read both). Actual unique reach likely 220-260M after audience deduplication.

insights.label: Efficiency Target
insights.text: Focus on reach efficiency improvement: same 352M reach but 2× engagement rate by Q4 2026 through better content quality, not volume expansion.

insights.label: Positive Gap
insights.text: SM: 41% positive vs TM: 5.8% positive. Gap is structural (audience self-selection) but can be partially closed through proactive TM editorial engagement and journalist relationship building.

insights.label: Neutral Contrast
insights.text: SM: 54% neutral vs TM: 71% neutral. TM defaults to factual/informational. Reducing TM neutral requires providing journalists with compelling angles that go beyond product announcements.

insights.label: Negative Comparison
insights.text: SM: 4% negative vs TM: 3.2% negative. Comparable levels. TM negative is concentrated in specialist trade outlets; SM negative distributed across platforms. Both manageable.

insights.label: Combined Brand Health
insights.text: ACME's media landscape is healthy — above-average positive rates, manageable negative, concentrated narrative around product and innovation. Strategic opportunity is positive rate improvement, not crisis management.

insights.label: Synchronization
insights.text: Both channels peak in February — confirming that product launch drives simultaneous SM and TM volume spikes. Channels amplified each other during the launch window.

insights.label: Scale Difference
insights.text: TM (2,600-3,100 articles/month) is ~7× SM volume (340-510 posts/month). TM provides structural coverage floor; SM provides engagement amplification layer.

insights.label: SM Volatility
insights.text: SM volume fluctuates more dramatically (+46% Jan-to-Feb) vs TM (+11%). SM is more event-responsive; TM is more structurally stable via wire syndication.

insights.label: Opportunity Windows
insights.text: Q2 2026 volume depends on new catalysts. Proactive pipeline: Q2 customer conference, product update release, enterprise partnership announcement. Pre-plan for each to create SM/TM synchronized moments.

insights.label: Innovation (8 SM / 7 TM)
insights.text: Strong across both channels. AI platform story and X1 launch are generating consistent innovation coverage. Sustain through continued AI content and R&D story pitching.

insights.label: Financial Strength (6 SM / 7 TM)
insights.text: TM-strong driven by analyst upgrade and revenue guidance. SM financial content is less developed. Executive investor communications on LinkedIn could strengthen SM financial score.

insights.label: Product Quality (7 SM / 6 TM)
insights.text: Good SM score from launch enthusiasm and customer testimonials. TM score impacted by supply chain narrative creating implicit quality concern. Resolution messaging will restore TM score.

insights.label: Customer Trust (5 SM / 4 TM)
insights.text: Moderate across both channels. Customer voice is present but not dominant in either channel. Building a structured customer advocacy content program would substantially lift both scores.

insights.label: TM Scale
insights.text: 8,500 TM articles vs 1,200 SM posts — TM is 7× SM by count. Normal for enterprise tech. TM's structural coverage ensures baseline brand visibility independent of SM activity.

insights.label: SM Quality Premium
insights.text: Despite 1/7th the volume, SM generates 9,840 interactions vs near-zero TM interactions. SM converts coverage to brand engagement and advocacy.

insights.label: Channel Synchronization
insights.text: Both channels peaked in February around the product launch — confirming that major announcements successfully activate both earned and organic media channels simultaneously.

insights.label: Implication
insights.text: ACME's SM content program should grow to 1,800-2,000 posts/quarter to match the professional brand presence of comparable enterprise technology companies.

uni-ch2.title: Narrative Gaps: What's Missing
uni-ch2.sub: Absent story angles across both channels

insights.label: Customer Voice
insights.text: Customer testimonials appear in SM but aren't driving TM earned editorial. Each enterprise customer win should be pitched as a TM story to trade outlets — customer success = third-party validation = credibility.

insights.label: Supply Chain Resolution
insights.text: The supply chain narrative is present in TM but no proactive resolution story has been published. This gap leaves the negative thread active. A proactive update would neutralize it.

insights.label: AI Platform Depth
insights.text: AI story has strong SM resonance but TM coverage is surface-level. A deep-dive editorial feature in MIT Technology Review or Wired on ACME's AI architecture would build lasting narrative authority.

insights.label: Employee Voice
insights.text: Employer brand content is minimal in both channels. Engineering blog posts, team milestone content, and culture stories on LinkedIn would improve talent acquisition narrative and employee advocacy.

insights.label: LinkedIn Priority
insights.text: LinkedIn is ACME's highest-ROI single channel at 8.2 interactions/post. Scale content investment here first — the audience is present, engaged, and converting to brand advocacy.

insights.label: Moment Amplification
insights.text: When TM breaks a major story (customer win, analyst upgrade, product milestone), ACME has a 6-12 hour window to amplify on LinkedIn/X before momentum fades. Process speed is the gap.

insights.label: Trade Media Cultivation
insights.text: TechCrunch (185 articles) is already an engaged outlet. Building a quarterly exclusive briefing relationship with TechCrunch and ZDNet would compound editorial coverage quality.

insights.label: Integrated Calendar
insights.text: Build an integrated 12-month content calendar mapping product milestones, customer stories, industry conferences, and earnings events. Coordinate SM and TM strategies in advance for each moment.

recommendations.title: Scale LinkedIn Content Investment
recommendations.description: LinkedIn delivers 8.2 interactions per post — above enterprise software benchmark. Increase posting frequency from current ~5.6 posts/day to 10+ posts/day through expanded content types: customer case studies, AI platform updates, executive thought leadership, and product feature explainers.
recommendations.effort: Medium — requires 1-2 content creators and a compliance review workflow
recommendations.impact: High — projected 2× interaction growth (from 4,133 to 8,000+ LinkedIn interactions/quarter)
actions: Hire or contract 2 LinkedIn content specialists with enterprise tech background by end of Q2
Launch a monthly 'ACME AI Insights' LinkedIn article series featuring use-case data and customer outcomes

recommendations.title: Resolve Supply Chain Narrative with Proactive Media Engagement
recommendations.description: 272 negative TM articles in Q1 are concentrated around the APAC supply chain disruption. A proactive resolution story pitched to Supply Chain Dive and Logistics Management — the primary negative hubs — would directly neutralize the narrative at its source.
recommendations.effort: Low — 2 weeks of PR work to develop and pitch the supply chain resilience story
recommendations.impact: High — could reduce TM negative rate from 3.2% to under 2% within 60 days
actions: Draft a supply chain resilience press release highlighting APAC recovery timeline and diversification measures by April 15
Schedule exclusive briefings with editors at Supply Chain Dive and Logistics Management for April 20-25

recommendations.title: Build AI Platform TM Narrative
recommendations.description: The AI platform announcement resonates strongly in SM (high engagement) but TM coverage is surface-level. Proactive pitching to AI-focused publications would build a TM AI narrative that matches SM enthusiasm and creates durable innovation positioning.
recommendations.effort: Medium — requires dedicated media relations for AI and tech publications
recommendations.impact: Medium — 10-15 Tier 1 AI publication placements per quarter could add 30M+ quality reach
actions: Identify and brief 5 AI/tech journalists (MIT Technology Review, Wired, The Verge) with exclusive AI platform technical deep-dive by Q3
Commission CTO byline for Harvard Business Review or McKinsey Quarterly on enterprise AI transformation — submission by end of Q2

recommendations.title: Competitor Launch Response Readiness
recommendations.description: CompetitorX is rumored to launch a competing product in April 2026. Without proactive differentiation content and a rapid-response communications plan, ACME's Q1 launch momentum could be absorbed by competitor coverage.
recommendations.effort: Low — communications planning exercise with existing team
recommendations.impact: High — prevents SOV erosion of up to 20-30% if competitor launch generates outsized coverage
actions: Conduct competitive differentiation audit by April 10 — map CompetitorX's likely messaging against ACME's proof points
Pre-draft 3 response narratives for competitor launch scenarios: feature comparison, customer retention story, analyst-facing differentiation brief

insights.label: High Priority (2)
insights.text: LinkedIn scale-up and supply chain narrative resolution are immediate, Q2-executable actions with high certainty of positive ROI. Both are addressable within 60 days.

insights.label: Medium Priority (2)
insights.text: AI platform TM narrative and competitor response readiness require planning but deliver sustained Q3-Q4 benefits. Begin Q2, execute Q3.

insights.label: Priority Order
insights.text: Recommended execution sequence: (1) Supply chain resolution — April (2 weeks). (2) LinkedIn scale-up — April hiring, May launch. (3) AI TM narrative — May-June pitching. (4) Competitor readiness — April planning, activate on trigger.

insights.label: Expected Outcome
insights.text: Full execution of all 4 recommendations by Q3 2026 should yield: TM positive rate 7-8%, SM interactions 14,000+/quarter, TM negative rate <2%, and maintained SOV against competitor launch.

insights.label: Why High Priority
insights.text: LinkedIn scale-up and supply chain narrative fix are the two actions with the clearest ROI certainty, fastest time-to-impact, and lowest execution risk. Both can show measurable results within 60-90 days.

insights.label: LinkedIn Investment Case
insights.text: Current LinkedIn performance (8.2 int/post, 504 posts/quarter) leaves significant volume-driven growth on the table. Adding 1-2 content creators is a low-cost, high-leverage investment.

insights.label: Supply Chain Urgency
insights.text: Q2 earnings will be the moment when supply chain revenue impact (if any) becomes public. Resolving the narrative BEFORE earnings is critical — reacting after earnings is a much harder communications problem.

recs-medium.title: Medium Priority Actions
recs-medium.sub: Plan in Q2, execute in Q3

insights.label: AI TM Narrative
insights.text: Building TM AI coverage requires journalist relationship development — a 60-90 day investment before results appear. Starting Q2 delivers Q3 coverage. Delay means competitors define the AI-in-enterprise narrative first.

insights.label: Competitor Readiness
insights.text: Pre-planning costs almost nothing and prevents communications paralysis if CompetitorX's launch generates significant coverage. The preparation asymmetry (low cost, high protection value) makes this a clear priority even at medium urgency.

insights.label: Sequencing
insights.text: Medium priority actions should not delay high priority execution. Run in parallel: assign supply chain/LinkedIn to PR team while communications strategy team handles competitive readiness and AI narrative planning.

insights.label: Evidence
insights.text: LinkedIn generates 4,133 interactions from 504 posts (8.2 int/post). Above enterprise benchmark of 6.0. The audience is engaged and converting to brand advocacy — the ROI case is proven.

insights.label: Content Mix
insights.text: Recommended split: 30% customer success stories, 25% product/AI thought leadership, 20% executive perspective, 15% industry data and insights, 10% team/culture content.

insights.label: Compliance
insights.text: Enterprise technology content has lower regulatory compliance burden than pharma/financial services. Standard legal review for IP and competitive claims. Build a 3-day review turnaround workflow.

insights.label: KPI
insights.text: Target: 1,000 LinkedIn posts/quarter (from current 504), 8,000+ interactions/quarter (from current 4,133), 8.5+ average interactions/post (maintain quality while scaling volume).

insights.label: Mechanism
insights.text: Supply chain negative coverage is concentrated in 2-3 trade outlets. A directly pitched resolution story to these specific outlets converts the negative hubs to neutral-to-positive. This is surgical narrative management.

insights.label: Story Angle
insights.text: Frame the story as 'ACME's supply chain resilience program': (1) What happened, (2) How ACME responded, (3) Structural changes implemented, (4) Customer impact prevented. Transparency builds credibility.

insights.label: Timing
insights.text: Publish before Q2 earnings (target: April 15-20). Financial journalists reviewing Q2 results will find the proactive supply chain update — rather than only the original disruption coverage.

insights.label: KPI
insights.text: Target: TM negative rate Q2 < 2% (from current 3.2%). Supply Chain Dive publishes at least 1 positive/neutral ACME supply chain resilience story by April 30.

insights.label: Context
insights.text: AI integration story generates 8.5+ interactions/post on LinkedIn — above-average engagement signal. Converting this SM enthusiasm to TM editorial coverage creates durable innovation positioning.

insights.label: Target Outlets
insights.text: MIT Technology Review (2.5M reach, technical credibility), Wired (4M reach, innovation narrative), The Verge (6M reach, consumer-to-enterprise bridge), VentureBeat (enterprise AI focus).

insights.label: Content Strategy
insights.text: Develop a 'ACME AI Architecture' series: (1) How ACME's AI works (technical credibility), (2) Customer outcomes data (proof), (3) Roadmap (forward momentum). One piece per outlet per quarter.

insights.label: KPI
insights.text: Target: 10+ Tier 1 AI/tech publication placements in Q3 2026. 'ACME AI' branded mentions in TM grow 3× from Q1 baseline by Q4 2026.

insights.label: Risk Scenario
insights.text: If CompetitorX launches in April 2026 with strong product features and generates >500 SM posts and >300 TM articles, ACME's Q1 SOV advantage could erode within 30 days. Preparation prevents reactive panic.

insights.label: Differentiation Pillars
insights.text: Pre-map ACME's 3 strongest differentiators vs. CompetitorX's likely messaging: (1) AI-native architecture, (2) Enterprise integration depth, (3) Customer success track record. Have data ready for each.

insights.label: Response Hierarchy
insights.text: Tier 1 response (major CompetitorX win): Executive LinkedIn post + analyst briefing within 24 hours. Tier 2 response (normal launch coverage): Competitive comparison content on owned channels within 72 hours.

insights.label: KPI
insights.text: ACME maintains >50% of combined SM conversation volume vs CompetitorX through Q2. Share of voice does not drop below 40% even during peak competitor launch coverage window.
